compiler_trust_notes: Roux now carries an accession-backed matrix-derived identity-pressure override from the single-cell MSC initiation snapshot.
Roux age still remains a manual benchmark field in this tranche, but it now carries explicit matrix support from aged-versus-young control anchors in the same accession-backed file.
The Roux file exposes only one lane per age_treatment group, so this tranche treats age movement as support-only rather than a direct override.
Roux remains initiation-phase and pseudotime-supported rather than duration-authoritative, so this tranche does not claim late safety or duration calibration.
Risk pressure remains manual because the initiation snapshot gives strong identity-escape evidence but not a clean outcome-grade hazard proxy.

matrix_source_locators: Aged_NegCtrl and Young_NegCtrl adult control groups
State annotations plus adult-axis departure geometry inside the accession-backed file
matrix_support_summaries: Aged treated Roux cells show a high reprogramming-state fraction (0.720) and near-max departure from the adult control age axis (0.989), which now directly derives the high identity-pressure label under the Roux initiation identity contract.

Aged treated Roux cells show only bounded youthful movement on the aged-control to young-control gene axis (0.182 support score; 0.243 weighted gene-direction concordance), so age_signal_score stays manual in this tranche rather than being silently overwritten.
Risk pressure remains manual because this initiation snapshot is strong for identity escape but not outcome-grade hazard truth.
